question: What alcoholic drinks are popular in Japan beyond sake?
answer: Beyond sake, quality spirits such as gin and whiskey are staples in Japan. In recent years, the craft beer revolution has also made a significant impact on the country's drinking culture.
source_title: Entertainment and Nightlife in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What is the cover charge custom I might encounter at an izakaya?
answer: Some izakayas levy a per-person cover charge on customers. In return, a small dish may be brought to you before you've even placed an order.
source_title: Entertainment and Nightlife in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What are the different ways to get up Mt. Hakodate?
answer: You can ascend Mt. Hakodate by ropeway, bus, taxi, or on foot. From the Jujigai tram stop, it's a 10-minute walk to the Mt. Hakodate Ropeway Station, and you can also take a bus from Hakodate Station or hike to the top in about an hour.
source_title: Hakodate Night View | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: When is the best time to see the night view from Mt. Hakodate?
answer: The best time is during blue hour, the hour after the sun sets. The city lights look their best about 30 minutes after sunset, though this is also when crowds are at their largest.
source_title: Hakodate Night View | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What is Goryokaku and why is it significant?
answer: Goryokaku is a star-shaped, Western-style fortress visible from Mt. Hakodate. It holds the distinction of being Japan's first Western-style fortress.
source_title: Hakodate Night View | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Can I visit Mt. Hakodate in winter and what makes each season special?
answer: Yes, Mt. Hakodate can be enjoyed throughout the year. Each season offers a unique perspective, with spring bringing seas of clouds, summer offering mysterious mists, autumn providing clear and sharp views, and winter showcasing the city gleaming under snow.
source_title: Hakodate Night View | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Is there anything special to look out for between June and December at Mt. Hakodate?
answer: Between June and December, visitors can look out for the lights of squid fishing boats on the water, adding an extra element to the night view.
source_title: Hakodate Night View | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What was the Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse originally used for?
answer: The Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse was originally built as a customs inspection house and served as temporary bonded warehouses for storing unprocessed goods brought in from overseas, when Yokohama was the primary trading port for the Tokyo area in the 19th century.
source_title: Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How long does it take to get to the Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse from Tokyo Station by JR train?
answer: From Tokyo Station, you can take JR to Sakuragicho in about 35 minutes, which is then a 15-minute walk from the Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse.
source_title: Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What kinds of things can visitors do at the Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse today?
answer: Visitors can enjoy outdoor markets and festivities at the Event Square, attend concerts or exhibitions in Warehouse No. 1, relax in Red Brick Park with views of the port, shop for clothing, Japanese stationery, and dishware, and dine at cafes or restaurants with sea views.
source_title: Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How many theme parks and amusement parks are there in Japan?
answer: Japan has more than 100 theme park and amusement park locations nationwide.
source_title: Theme Parks and Amusement Parks | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What makes Tokyo Disneyland's Monsters, Inc. attraction unique compared to other Disney parks?
answer: Tokyo Disneyland's 'Monsters, Inc. Ride and Go Seek!' is interactive, allowing riders to use a flashlight to spot hiding monsters, making it different from Monsters, Inc. attractions at other Disney parks.
source_title: Theme Parks and Amusement Parks | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What are some rides that can only be experienced at Tokyo DisneySea?
answer: Exclusive attractions at Tokyo DisneySea include Sindbad's Storybook Voyage, Tower of Terror, Journey to the Center of the Earth, 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea, and Raging Spirits.
source_title: Theme Parks and Amusement Parks | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: When did Universal Studios Japan open, and what was significant about it?
answer: Universal Studios Japan opened in 2001 and was the first Universal Studios theme park to open in Asia. It features 10 areas and includes attractions such as THE WIZARDING WORLD OF HARRY POTTER™ and the Japan-exclusive SUPER NINTENDO WORLD™.
source_title: Theme Parks and Amusement Parks | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What kind of world records does Fuji-Q Highland hold?
answer: Fuji-Q Highland holds world records for roller coasters with the most inversions, fastest acceleration, and steepest drop, making it a paradise for thrill seekers.
source_title: Theme Parks and Amusement Parks | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: Is there anything to do at Fuji-Q Highland if I decide not to ride the roller coasters?
answer: Yes, if you skip the roller coasters, you can enjoy stunning views of Mt. Fuji on a clear day, and there are also more mellow rides suitable for children.
source_title: Theme Parks and Amusement Parks | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What characters can visitors expect to see at the Hello Kitty-themed park in Japan?
answer: Visitors can meet Hello Kitty along with popular friends like Cinnamoroll and Pompompurin, as well as other lesser-known characters, and there are plenty of opportunities to purchase character merchandise.
source_title: Theme Parks and Amusement Parks | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: How do I get to Iga from Osaka or Tokyo?
answer: Both JR and Kintetsu train lines connect Iga to Osaka, Kyoto, and Nagoya. JR runs to Iga-Ueno Station, while Kintetsu serves Iga-Kambe Station, and from either stop you can take a train to downtown Iga's Uenoshi Station. The total journey from Osaka, Kyoto, or Nagoya takes between 90 minutes and two hours.
source_title: Iga | Mie | Tokai | Destinations | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What can I do at the Ninja Museum of Igaryu?
answer: At the Ninja Museum of Igaryu, you can explore a maze-like ninja house, watch ninjutsu masters demonstrate their techniques, and even test your own skills by throwing razor-sharp ninja weapons.
source_title: Iga | Mie | Tokai | Destinations | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Is there a place near Iga where I can try actual ninja training?
answer: Yes, the Akame 48 Waterfalls area in the secluded mountains south of Iga City offers a ninja training course held on the same terrain where ninja once practiced their arts.
source_title: Iga | Mie | Tokai | Destinations | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What is there to do at Yunoyama Onsen besides hot springs?
answer: Besides relaxing in hot spring baths at one of the local inns, visitors can take a ropeway to the summit of Mt. Gozaisho, where skiing, hiking trails, and panoramic views are available.
source_title: Iga | Mie | Tokai | Destinations | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Can I see a Japanese giant salamander when visiting the Akame 48 Waterfalls?
answer: Yes, you can look for the rare Japanese giant salamander along the river while hiking at Akame 48 Waterfalls. If you don't spot one in the wild, the Japanese Salamander Center near the entrance to the falls guarantees a sighting.
source_title: Iga | Mie | Tokai | Destinations | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How tall is Tokyo Skytree and can you see Mt. Fuji from it?
answer: Tokyo Skytree stands 634 meters tall, making it the tallest structure in Japan. On a clear day, it is one of the few spots in Tokyo from which you can see Mt. Fuji.
source_title: Tokyo Skytree | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How do I get to Tokyo Skytree by train?
answer: You can reach Tokyo Skytree by taking the Tobu Skytree Line from Asakusa to Tokyo Skytree Station, which drops you right at Tokyo Skytree Town. Alternatively, the Asakusa, Hanzomon, or Keisei Oshiage lines will take you to nearby Oshiage Station, or you can walk from Asakusa in about 20 minutes.
source_title: Tokyo Skytree | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What observation decks are available at Tokyo Skytree and what can visitors do there?
answer: Tokyo Skytree has two public observation decks: the Tembo Deck at 350 meters and the Tembo Galleria at 450 meters, both offering panoramic views from morning to night. Thrill-seekers can walk on the glass floor of the Tembo Deck or the glass-walled Tembo Galleria for the sensation of floating in mid-air.
source_title: Tokyo Skytree | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How tall is Mt. Fuji?
answer: Mt. Fuji stands at 3,776 meters, making it Japan's most iconic and highest peak.
source_title: Mt. Fuji Climbing Guide | Mt. Fuji Hike | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What is the mandatory hiking fee to climb Mt. Fuji?
answer: As of 2025, a mandatory hiking fee of 4,000 yen per person is required to climb Mt. Fuji.
source_title: Mt. Fuji Climbing Guide | Mt. Fuji Hike | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: When is the Mt. Fuji climbing season open?
answer: The climbing season runs from early July to early September, with peak season lasting from late July to late August.
source_title: Mt. Fuji Climbing Guide | Mt. Fuji Hike | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Which trail is best for beginner hikers on Mt. Fuji?
answer: The Yoshida Trail is the most popular and accessible route, making it ideal for beginners. It has many facilities and a separate descent route.
source_title: Mt. Fuji Climbing Guide | Mt. Fuji Hike | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What are the access restrictions for climbing Mt. Fuji?
answer: Entry is permitted for all climbers between 3am and 2pm. Access between 2pm and 3am is restricted to visitors who have mountain hut reservations.
source_title: Mt. Fuji Climbing Guide | Mt. Fuji Hike | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What is the temperature like at the summit of Mt. Fuji in summer?
answer: Even in mid-summer, the temperature at the summit is between approximately five and eight degrees Celsius, so layers and warm clothing are essential.
source_title: Mt. Fuji Climbing Guide | Mt. Fuji Hike | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What is the best strategy for catching the sunrise from the top of Mt. Fuji?
answer: Many hikers opt for a two-day journey, resting at a mountain hut halfway up before departing in the early hours of the morning to reach the summit in time for sunrise.
source_title: Mt. Fuji Climbing Guide | Mt. Fuji Hike | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Do I need a visa to visit Japan as a tourist?
answer: It depends on your nationality. Passport holders from certain countries do not need a visa if their stay is 90 days or less and they are only engaging in tourism activities. You should check the List of Countries and Regions that have Visa Exemption Arrangements with Japan for your specific country.
source_title: Japan Visa Requirements | Japan Travel Visa Information | JNTO

question: How long does it take to process a tourist visa for Japan?
answer: Processing times vary, but tourist visas for Japan generally take around five business days.
source_title: Japan Visa Requirements | Japan Travel Visa Information | JNTO

question: What are the restrictions on what I can do while staying in Japan on a tourist visa?
answer: While in Japan on a short-term tourist stay, you cannot engage in paid work, enroll in long-term educational programs, or apply for residency. Most short-term stays allow up to 90 days, though some nationalities may be granted 15, 30, or 180 days.
source_title: Japan Visa Requirements | Japan Travel Visa Information | JNTO

question: What is a shukubo?
answer: A shukubo is a traditional temple lodging in Japan that is open to visitors. Originally used by traveling monks, they eventually opened their doors to regular travelers seeking accommodation.
source_title: Overnight stay at a temple | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What kind of food is served at temple lodgings in Japan?
answer: Temple meals are called Shojin ryori, or Buddhist cuisine. They do not include meat, fish, dairy, or egg products, making them very similar to vegan dishes.
source_title: Overnight stay at a temple | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: Does the room price at a shukubo include meals?
answer: Yes, the room price at a shukubo typically includes both dinner and breakfast.
source_title: Overnight stay at a temple | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What should I know about payment when booking a shukubo stay?
answer: Some temples only accept cash, so it is best to confirm the payment method in advance before booking your stay.
source_title: Overnight stay at a temple | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: Can I drink alcohol during a shukubo stay?
answer: Alcohol policies vary by shukubo — some allow it as a medicinal food or drink, while others strictly forbid it. It is best to check with the specific shukubo in advance.
source_title: Overnight stay at a temple | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What does the Japanese term 'minpaku' mean?
answer: Minpaku means staying at someone's home in Japan. It refers to accommodations where guests can share a home with a family or rent an entire house or condominium.
source_title: Countryside Stays in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What is 'nouhaku' and what activities does it involve?
answer: Nouhaku means staying in a rural area while enjoying activities like farming, forestry, and fishing. It is a type of countryside stay that immerses guests in traditional rural Japanese life.
source_title: Countryside Stays in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: How do minpaku accommodations compare to hotels in terms of cost and space?
answer: Minpaku accommodations are typically roomier and more affordable than hotels. They often include the cleaning fee in the total room fee, though some room types may require guests to cook for themselves.
source_title: Countryside Stays in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What is a 'kominka' and where can you find one?
answer: A kominka is a traditional and quaint Japanese house found in the countryside, known in Japanese as the 'inaka.' It is one of the accommodation options available for those looking to experience historic Japanese architecture.
source_title: Countryside Stays in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: How can I make sure a minpaku I book is legally registered?
answer: You can find legally run minpaku on official websites such as Airbnb or STAY JAPAN. It is important to be aware that some non-registered minpaku do exist, so using these official platforms helps ensure legitimacy.
source_title: Countryside Stays in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What are some etiquette rules I should follow when staying at a minpaku?
answer: Guests should sort and dispose of garbage correctly, respect local rules and customs, and avoid making loud noise that could disturb neighboring residents. Since many minpaku are located in residential areas, being considerate of the surrounding community is essential.
source_title: Countryside Stays in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: Which regions in Japan are mentioned as good destinations for countryside stays?
answer: The northern part of Honshu and the Tohoku region, including Akita, are highlighted for traditional rural farm stays. Kyoto is also mentioned as an option, where guests can stay in farmhouses in the suburbs or eco-homes in the countryside.
source_title: Countryside Stays in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: How far is Narita Airport from Tokyo city center?
answer: Narita Airport is about an hour-long express train ride from Tokyo, as it is located in the neighboring prefecture of Chiba.
source_title: Getting to Japan | Japan Flights | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: How long does it take to get from Haneda Airport to central Tokyo?
answer: Visitors can reach the bustling city of Tokyo within half an hour of leaving the Haneda Airport terminal.
source_title: Getting to Japan | Japan Flights | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Which airport should I use if I want to visit Kyoto or Osaka?
answer: Kansai International Airport (KIX) serves the western area of Japan, including Kyoto and Osaka. It is located around 50 minutes from Shin-Osaka and 75 minutes from Kyoto Station.
source_title: Getting to Japan | Japan Flights | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Can I travel to Japan by ferry from South Korea?
answer: Yes, ferries travel between cities in South Korea, such as Busan, and Japanese cities like Kyushu and Osaka. For example, a high-speed ferry from Hakata to Busan takes about 3 hours 40 minutes for around 16,000 yen, while the Kampu Ferry to Osaka takes 12 hours for under 10,000 yen.
source_title: Getting to Japan | Japan Flights | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Which international airports in Japan can I fly into besides Tokyo?
answer: Besides Tokyo's airports, international flights are also handled by Osaka's Kansai International Airport, as well as airports in Naha (Okinawa) and Fukuoka, among others throughout the country.
source_title: Getting to Japan | Japan Flights | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: How reliable is public transportation in Japan?
answer: Japan's public transportation is known for its pinpoint punctuality, with timetables followed religiously across shinkansen, local trains, buses, and long-distance coaches, offering a service you can set your watch by.
source_title: Transportation in japan | Plan | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Are there ways to save money on travel within Japan?
answer: Yes, Japan offers a vast array of discount passes and travel cards that provide wallet-friendly options, allowing travelers to explore farther and wider with convenience and ease.
source_title: Transportation in japan | Plan | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Which shinkansen route connects Tokyo with Kyoto and Osaka?
answer: The Tokaido Shinkansen links Tokyo with Shin-Yokohama, Nagoya, Kyoto, and Shin-Osaka.
source_title: Shinkansen (Bullet Train) | Transportation | Plan | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What are the three types of trains on the Tokaido-Sanyo Shinkansen?
answer: The three types are Nozomi, Hikari, and Kodama. The Nozomi makes the fewest stops and is the quickest option for reaching the Kansai region.
source_title: Shinkansen (Bullet Train) | Transportation | Plan | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Can I use my Japan Rail Pass on the Nozomi Shinkansen?
answer: The Nozomi is not covered by the standard JAPAN RAIL PASS. However, pass holders can use a Nozomi train by additionally purchasing an [ONLY WITH JAPAN RAIL PASS] NOZOMI MIZUHO Ticket each time they board.
source_title: Shinkansen (Bullet Train) | Transportation | Plan | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Which shinkansen lines offer GranClass premier class seating?
answer: The Tohoku, Hokkaido, Hokuriku, and Joetsu Shinkansen lines feature GranClass premier class seats. Rail pass users must purchase an additional ticket to access GranClass.
source_title: Shinkansen (Bullet Train) | Transportation | Plan | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What shinkansen line would I take to travel from Hakata to Kagoshima?
answer: The Kyushu Shinkansen links Hakata with Kagoshima Chuo.
source_title: Shinkansen (Bullet Train) | Transportation | Plan | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How does the Hokkaido Shinkansen connect to the rest of Japan's rail network?
answer: The Hokkaido Shinkansen links Shin-Aomori with Shin-Hakodate-Hokuto, connecting Hokkaido to the Tohoku region of Honshu.
source_title: Shinkansen (Bullet Train) | Transportation | Plan | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Is there a shinkansen route that connects Nagasaki to other cities?
answer: Yes, the Nishi Kyushu Shinkansen links Nagasaki with Takeo-Onsen.
source_title: Shinkansen (Bullet Train) | Transportation | Plan | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What is the difference between a local train and a rapid train in Japan?
answer: Local trains stop at all stations and are ideal for short distances, while rapid trains skip minor stations but do not require an extra fare.
source_title: Traveling by Rail | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Which private railway should I take to get to Hakone from Tokyo?
answer: The Odakyu railway line provides direct access to Hakone, making it a convenient option for travelers heading to that destination.
source_title: Traveling by Rail | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What payment options can I use to ride trains in Japan?
answer: You can use IC travel cards like Suica, Pasmo, or ICOCA for tap-and-go convenience, mobile IC apps such as Welcome Suica Mobile or PASMO on compatible phones, or traditional paper tickets available at stations.
source_title: Traveling by Rail | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How can I look up train timetables and fares before my trip?
answer: You can use online tools like Japan Transit Planner (JORUDAN) or NAVITIME, where you enter your location and travel time to get convenient routes and up-to-date fare information.
source_title: Traveling by Rail | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Are there any fun scenic or themed trains in Japan worth riding just for the experience?
answer: Yes, Japan offers many scenic and themed trains designed purely for enjoyment, featuring incredible views and unique onboard experiences across various regions of the country.
source_title: Traveling by Rail | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How often do trains come in major Japanese cities?
answer: In major cities, trains come every few minutes. However, in rural areas, services can be more limited and require a little more planning.
source_title: How to travel by train and bus in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What train lines operate in Tokyo?
answer: In Tokyo, you'll find Japan Railways (JR), Tokyo Metro Lines, and Toei Subway Lines, among others.
source_title: How to travel by train and bus in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What apps can I use to find train routes and fares in Japan?
answer: You can use apps like Japan Travel by NAVITIME, Japan Transit Planner by Jorudan, and Google Maps to find routes and calculate fares.
source_title: How to travel by train and bus in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What is the deposit fee for most IC cards in Japan?
answer: The deposit fee for most IC cards is 500 yen. You can get your money back by returning the card to the ticket counter at the end of your stay.
source_title: How to travel by train and bus in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What is a Welcome Suica card and who is it for?
answer: Welcome Suica is a deposit-free IC card designed for tourists, available in certain places in the Kanto area. It is valid for up to 28 days.
source_title: How to travel by train and bus in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How do I add money to my IC card in Japan?
answer: You can add money at automatic ticket machines found at almost all train stations in Japan. Simply insert your card, follow the instructions, and select the amount you'd like to add.
source_title: How to travel by train and bus in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Can I use my IC card on highway buses in Japan?
answer: No, certain forms of transport such as highway buses do not accept IC cards. You should look for the IC logo to confirm whether your card is accepted.
source_title: How to travel by train and bus in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: What IC cards are commonly used in the Kanto area of Japan?
answer: Suica and PASMO are among the most commonly used IC cards in the Kanto area. There are also other cards like ICOCA and TOICA used in different areas of Japan.
source_title: How to travel by train and bus in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: Can I use IC cards for purchases other than transportation in Japan?
answer: Yes, IC cards can also be used to pay for items at many vending machines, stores, and lockers in train stations throughout Japan.
source_title: How to travel by train and bus in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site)

question: How does Japan's size compare to other countries or regions?
answer: Japan is often compared in size to Germany, being slightly smaller than the state of California and somewhat larger than the United Kingdom.
source_title: Weather & Geography | Japan's Weather | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What percentage of Japan's land is covered by forest and mountains?
answer: About 70 percent of Japan's land is made up of dense forest and mountainous terrain, held in place by the Japan Alps in central Honshu.
source_title: Weather & Geography | Japan's Weather | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Which countries are Japan's closest neighbors?
answer: Japan's closest neighbors are South Korea, Russia, and Taiwan, with Taiwan being near the southernmost Okinawan islands known as Yonaguni.
source_title: Weather & Geography | Japan's Weather | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: When do cherry blossoms typically bloom in Japan?
answer: Cherry blossoms bloom between March and May depending on location, starting in the south and gradually moving north across the country.
source_title: Weather & Geography | Japan's Weather | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What should travelers be cautious about when visiting Japan in late summer?
answer: Travelers should be aware that August and September may bring typhoons that can disrupt travel plans. The summer season is also stiflingly hot and intensely humid, so staying hydrated is important.
source_title: Weather & Geography | Japan's Weather | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Are there good beaches and water activities available in Japan?
answer: Yes, Japan has many beautiful beaches with great surfing spots on peninsulas such as the Boso, Izu, and Kii Peninsulas, as well as many places to snorkel and dive, especially further south in Okinawa.
source_title: Weather & Geography | Japan's Weather | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: How far are the Ogasawara Islands from Tokyo?
answer: The Ogasawara Islands are located approximately 1,000 kilometers south of Tokyo in the Pacific Ocean and are UNESCO World Heritage accredited.
source_title: Weather & Geography | Japan's Weather | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What is the typical weather like in Japan during spring?
answer: Most of Japan rests at a pleasant 12 degrees Celsius during spring, allowing comfortable travel in light outerwear and sweaters. However, temperatures may suddenly drop, so bringing a few extra layers is recommended.
source_title: Spring in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Which ski resorts in Hokkaido are good for skiing in spring?
answer: Skiers and snowboarders can head to Niseko, Furano, or Rusutsu in Hokkaido. Niseko features four connected resorts, Rusutsu offers an expansive resort with an amusement park and onsen hot springs, and Furano also has activities like snow rafting and snowmobiling.
source_title: Spring in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: When do cherry blossoms start blooming in Japan and where do they begin?
answer: Cherry blossom blooms begin from the southern tip of Kyushu in late March and gradually extend northwards through Honshu and Hokkaido into April. Southern cities like Fukuoka are among the first to see the blooms.
source_title: Spring in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What is Golden Week and should I avoid traveling to Japan during that time?
answer: Golden Week, beginning at the end of April, is a series of consecutive national holidays when most of Japan goes on vacation. Travelers are advised to consider planning their trips to avoid this peak travel period.
source_title: Spring in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Where can I see cherry blossoms in Tokyo?
answer: In Tokyo, cherry blossoms bloom in parks like Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden and Ueno Park. You can also find inspiring urban views along the Meguro River in Nakameguro and at Chidori-ga-fuchi Moat near the Imperial Palace.
source_title: Spring in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: When is the climbing season for Mt. Fuji?
answer: The Mt. Fuji climbing season begins in early July and continues to around the first week of September. Visitors should book accommodations well in advance as spots fill up quickly during this limited season.
source_title: Summer in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What is the Nagaoka Matsuri and where is it held?
answer: The Nagaoka Matsuri is a two-day fireworks festival held in the first week of August in Niigata Prefecture, ranked among the top three best fireworks displays in Japan. It typically features around 20,000 fireworks along the banks of the Shinano River.
source_title: Summer in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What traditional foods should I try during summer in Japan?
answer: During summer in Japan, you should try yakiniku BBQ, chilled somen noodles, and ice-cold beer. These are classic seasonal foods enjoyed by locals during the hot summer months.
source_title: Summer in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What is Obon and when does it take place?
answer: Obon is a traditional Japanese ritual held in mid-August that involves rest, family reunions, and regional festivals and customs. Many Japanese people return to their hometowns during this period to take part in the celebrations.
source_title: Summer in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What water sports can I enjoy in Japan during summer?
answer: Japan offers a wide range of summer water sports, including kayaking and river rafting in Yamanashi Prefecture, windsurfing in Kanagawa, surfing in Chiba, Wakayama, or Miyazaki, and snorkeling or scuba diving in Okinawa and its nearby islands.
source_title: Summer in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What is the Shinmei Fireworks Festival and where does it take place?
answer: The Shinmei Fireworks Festival is held in Yamanashi Prefecture on the northern side of Mt. Fuji, near the Fuefuki River and Kamanashi River. It features 20,000 fireworks synchronized to music and pyrotechnic displays, making it a great addition to a visit to Mt. Fuji.
source_title: Summer in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What is the weather like in Japan during summer?
answer: Summer in Japan is characterized by hot and humid weather, particularly throughout much of Honshu and Kyushu. Visitors are advised to pack light, breathable clothing to stay comfortable in the heat.
source_title: Summer in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: When do the leaves change color in Japan during autumn?
answer: From mid-November to early December, the trees turn vivid hues of red, orange, and yellow.
source_title: Autumn in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What is the weather like in Hokkaido during autumn?
answer: For most of the autumn season, Hokkaido is cold enough to require a winter jacket, as temperatures cool significantly further north.
source_title: Autumn in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Which garden in Tokyo is recommended for viewing autumn colors?
answer: The Rikugien Garden, a traditional Japanese garden, is recommended in Tokyo for its striking autumn views.
source_title: Autumn in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What flowers bloom in Japan during autumn?
answer: During autumn, you can find chrysanthemum, spider lily, and cosmos flowers colorfully displayed across Japan.
source_title: Autumn in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What is the Nagasaki Kunchi Festival and when does it take place?
answer: The Nagasaki Kunchi Festival is an autumn harvest celebration said to have started in 1634, held in the beginning of October. It features mikoshi float processions, Chinese-influenced dragon dances, and Dutch-inspired rituals reflecting the historical presence of Dutch traders near Nagasaki.
source_title: Autumn in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Why is Kyoto considered a great destination for autumn leaf viewing?
answer: Kyoto is considered a perfect destination for autumn leaf viewing due to its abundance of internationally renowned temples and shrines, such as Kiyomizudera Temple, which offer spectacular seasonal views.
source_title: Autumn in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Is there a good place near Mt. Fuji to enjoy autumn scenery?
answer: Lake Kawaguchiko at the base of Mt. Fuji is an excellent spot, where you can enjoy views of Mt. Fuji reflected in the serene lake waters with crimson trees along the banks and surrounding mountainsides.
source_title: Autumn in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What are some popular winter activities to enjoy in Japan?
answer: In winter, Japan offers snow festivals, skiing, snowboarding, and relaxing onsen resorts. You can also enjoy seasonal cuisine like nabe hot pots and ramen broths.
source_title: Winter in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Where should Tokyo-based travelers go for skiing in winter?
answer: Tokyo-based travelers typically head to nearby Nagano for great skiing and convenient accessibility. Hakuba is one popular resort choice among several others in the area.
source_title: Winter in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Which part of Japan offers the freshest powder and biggest scale for winter snow sports?
answer: Hokkaido, the northern island, offers the freshest powder and biggest scale for winter sports, with plenty of open space and rolling mountain terrain. Niseko and Rusutsu are common destinations for ski tourists there.
source_title: Winter in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Where can travelers in the Kansai region go for a winter ski day trip?
answer: Travelers in the Kansai region, including Osaka, Kyoto, Nara, and Kobe, can take an easy day trip to Biwako Valley in the mountains surrounding Lake Biwa.
source_title: Winter in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What is hatsumode and when does it take place?
answer: Hatsumode is the traditional Japanese practice of visiting a temple or shrine on the first day of the New Year to pray for good fortune and success. It is a popular way to celebrate the holiday, as most other attractions are closed during this time.
source_title: Winter in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What is the top hatsumode destination in Tokyo?
answer: Meiji-jingu Shrine is the top hatsumode destination in Tokyo. It features a serene forested walking path that fills with visitors making their way to the main hall.
source_title: Winter in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Should I be concerned about restaurants and attractions being closed during my winter trip to Japan?
answer: Yes, during the New Year's holidays, most restaurants and attractions shut down in Japan. It is recommended to plan ahead to avoid any disappointments during this period.
source_title: Winter in Japan | Guide | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Do I need to carry my passport with me while traveling around Japan?
answer: Yes, you should carry your passport with you at all times as a form of identification. While you likely won't need to show it often, you may be required to present it at any time.
source_title: Local Laws | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Can I smoke freely in restaurants and bars in Japan?
answer: No, smoking is not allowed in most restaurants and bars except in designated areas. This rule is strictly enforced, especially in major cities, and violations can result in a fine.
source_title: Local Laws | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Will I be denied entry to onsen or public baths if I have tattoos in Japan?
answer: Many public swimming pools, public baths, hot spring resorts, and gyms in Japan prohibit entry to anyone with tattoos due to their historical association with crime. However, rules are loosening slightly, and some establishments may simply ask you to cover your tattoos, so it's best to research specific venues in advance.
source_title: Local Laws | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Is the JR Rail Pass worth it for traveling around Japan on a budget?
answer: The JR Rail Pass is recommended if you plan to do extensive traveling throughout Japan. However, if you plan to stay in one region, regional passes may offer better value for money.
source_title: Tips for Budget Travels | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What budget airlines can I use to fly within Japan?
answer: Several budget airlines operate in Japan, including Zip Air, Peach Air, JetStar Japan, and Spring Japan. Japan's main carriers, ANA and JAL, also offer cheaper flights for visitors.
source_title: Tips for Budget Travels | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: How can I save on both transportation and accommodation costs at the same time in Japan?
answer: Taking an overnight bus is a great way to save on both costs, as it covers long-distance travel while also eliminating the need to pay for a night's accommodation. From the Tokyo area, overnight buses run to destinations like Osaka, Kyoto, Hiroshima, Akita, and Aomori.
source_title: Tips for Budget Travels | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What are some affordable accommodation options available in Japan?
answer: Budget travelers can choose from business hotels, capsule hotels, and boutique hostels, which offer individuality at hostel prices. Traditional ryokans can also be found at lower prices in more out-of-the-way locations.
source_title: Tips for Budget Travels | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: Are there ways to enjoy cultural activities in Japan without spending much money?
answer: Many temples, shrines, and parks in Japan are free or charge only a few hundred yen. In Tokyo, the Grutt Pass provides discounted admission to many attractions and museums, making it a great option for budget travelers.
source_title: Tips for Budget Travels | Travel Japan | JNTO

question: What is considered Japan's most popular sport among locals?
answer: While sumo is Japan's national sport, baseball is arguably Japan's most popular sport in Japan.
source_title: Explore Japan's Stories & Guides | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: Is ramen a good budget-friendly food option when traveling in Japan?
answer: Yes, ramen in Japan is inexpensive, convenient, fun, and most importantly, delicious, making it a great option for budget-conscious travelers.
source_title: Explore Japan's Stories & Guides | Travel Japan（Japan National Tourism Organization）

question: What does accessible tourism in Japan include and who is it designed for?
answer: Accessible tourism is travel designed for everyone, including the elderly, people with disabilities, families with children, and anyone else who may need assistance. Japan strives to enable anyone, regardless of age or physical ability, to explore and travel throughout the country worry-free.
source_title: Accessible Tourism | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: How many barrier-free outdoor courses are available in Tokyo for sightseeing?
answer: There are 30 listed barrier-free courses available at sightseeing destinations across Tokyo. These allow visitors to explore neighborhoods like Shinjuku, Tsukiji, and Ueno.
source_title: Accessible Tourism | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What kinds of attractions can I visit in Yokohama as an accessible tourism destination?
answer: In Yokohama, you can visit sights like Minato Mirai 21, shop in the Minatomirai area, explore the Cupnoodles Museum Yokohama, or catch a baseball game at Nissan Stadium. Yokohama is located just south of Tokyo in Kanagawa.
source_title: Accessible Tourism | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What activities can I enjoy at Lake Suwa in Nagano?
answer: At Lake Suwa in Nagano, you can enjoy hot springs, art, culture, and more. Fans of the anime 'Your Name' may also recognize the scenery, as Lake Suwa resembles locations featured in the film.
source_title: Accessible Tourism | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: Can I find pre-planned accessible itineraries for visiting Tokyo?
answer: Yes, ready-made itineraries with all accessibility bases covered are available for Tokyo. These help travelers spend less time planning and more time enjoying what Tokyo has to offer.
source_title: Accessible Tourism | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: What types of accessible sports activities are available in Japan?
answer: Sports facilities in Japan are becoming more accessible, allowing people of all ages and abilities to enjoy activities on land, in the sea, or through the sky in any season. Information is also available on Japanese parasports and athletic prosthetics.
source_title: Accessible Tourism | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)

question: Where can I find information about using public transportation accessibly in Tokyo?
answer: Accessible tourism guides for Tokyo provide information on how and when to use public transportation like trains, buses, and taxis. These guides also cover lodging, restrooms, and how to access popular tourist attractions.
source_title: Accessible Tourism | Guide | Travel Japan (Japan National Tourism Organization)
